name: Asbury, Thomas Lyne
motto: "Taught or untaught, the dunce is still the same."

name: Austin, Won Ting
motto: "Who does the best his circumstance allows, does well; acts nobly; angels could do no more."

name: Bailey, Ernest Aubrey
motto: "With blinded eyesight poring over miserable books."

name: George Barnes Barrett
motto: "Whence, glaring oft with many a broad-en'd orb, he frights the nations."

name: Enoch Blassingame Benson
motto: "Benson and Blackmar, one and insep-arable, now and forever."

name: Aaron Blum Bernd
motto: "Is it the lark that sings so out of tune, straining harsh discords and unpleasing sharps?"

name: Dana B. Blackmar
motto: "Benson and Blackmar, one and inseparable, now and forever."

name: Charles McDonald Brown
motto: "'Tis but a freckle,—never mind it."

name: Clarence Avery Bryant
motto: "His corn and cattle were his only care, And his supreme delight a county fair."

name: James Young Bowen
motto: "Zooks, sir; flesh and blood, That's all I'm made of."

name: Charles Buchwald
motto: "With volleys of eternal babble, And clamour more unanswerable."

name: Frank W. Cheney
motto: "You may relish him more in the soldier than in the scholar."

name: Arthur Gibbes Crafts
motto: "Length without breadth is not good measure."

name: Jefferson Irwin Davis
motto: "Ambition is no cure for love."

name: Edward Carleton Dillard
motto: "As some to church repair, Not for the doctrine, but the music there."

name: Samuel Boyce Fisher
motto: "Powder thy radiant hair."

name: Bob Jake Fletcher
motto: "A great sweet silence."

name: Mason J. Florence
motto: "In every rank of great or small, 'Tis industry supports us all."

name: Casper Ira Funkenstein
motto: "For me, I think I speak as I was taught."

name: Tinnie Rucker Ginn
motto: "It's easier to slide than to climb."

name: Claude Luke Gray
motto: "Hope springs eternal in the human breast."

name: Frank Alexander Holden
motto: "All the Latin I construe is 'amo', I love."

name: Julian Cooper Howard
motto: "Direct not him whose way himself will choose, 'Tis breath thou lack'st, and that breath wilt thou lose."

name: James Augustus Johnston
motto: "By the Lord, lads, I'm glad you have the money."

name: Fred Capers Jordan
motto: "Ye Gods! but he is wondrous fair."

name: Dewitt Wilson Loyd
motto: "The field To labor calls us, now with sweat imposed."

name: Burley Matthew Lufburrow
motto: "Wherefore should I care?"

name: Henry Towns Maddox
motto: "As if man were created to no use But only to show his teeth."

name: Clarence Ellwood Martin
motto: "Be merry; and employ your chiefest thoughts to courtship."

name: Thomas Allan Maxwell
motto: "So sound he slept that nought might him awake."

name: Merry, Pierce
motto: "Thy beauty,---not a fault is there."

name: Michael, Leroy
motto: "Hum! Thou art a soldier."

name: Middlebrooks, Chauncey V.
motto: "He shows occasionally surface indications of intellect."

name: Francis Marion Moise
motto: "He's a rare linguist."

name: Joseph Myers
motto: "How does thy honor? Let me lick thy shoe."

name: Charles B. McDaniel, Jr.
motto: "Il n'y qu'un Français de plus."

name: John Edward McDonald
motto: "Let's to billiards."

name: David Knox McKamy
motto: "You'd scarce expect one of my age, To speak in public on the stage."

name: Robert Ligon McWhorter
motto: "Is this the face that stopped a thousand clocks?"

name: William Clyde Nanney
motto: "He studiously surveyed his generous vines."

name: Edward Barbara O'Kelley
motto: "The fruit that can fall without shaking Indeed is too good for me."

name: Everette Pattman
motto: "A cat may look at a king."

name: Russell Hugo Patterson
motto: "I awoke one day and found myself famous."

name: Erle Ewart Peacock
motto: "But riches purchased wisdom yet for none."

name: Henry McDaniel Pitts
motto: "What's to become of my despised time?"

name: Lennie Groover Proctor
motto: "A man would be great and he asked of his soul, I prithee what shall I do to be great?"

name: Edgar Rudolf Pund
motto: "I have caught An everlasting cold; I have lost my voice Most irrecoverably."

name: Richard Newell Schwab
motto: "All his reverent wit lies in his wardrobe."

name: Robert Dunham Short
motto: "Might not a child of good discretion Be leader to an army?"

name: Guy Summerour
motto: "He is so quiet, that he seems to sleep The tempest out, as dormice do in winter."

name: Paul Tabor
motto: "Honors come by diligence; riches spring from economy."

name: Theodore Markwalter Von Sprecken
motto: "You should not take a fellow eight years old And make him swear to never kiss the girls."

name: John Donald Wade
motto: "When I was young, I gave my mind And plied myself to fruitless poetry."

name: Edison Collins Westbrook
motto: "He swore that he would never leave those old red hills and stones."

name: Hoyt Henry Whelchel
motto: "To-morrow, to-morrow, not to-day, Hear the lazy people say."

name: Olin J. Wimberley
motto: "A lion among ladies is a most dangerous thing."

name: Alphonse Anderson Winter
motto: "'Tis bitter cold and I am sick at heart."

name: Arturo Diaz
motto: "Gad, he's an engineer."

name: Robert O. Suddeth
motto: "Void of college honors, offices, or obligations."

name: Thomas Davenport Symmes
motto: "Ye've naught to do but mark and tell Your neighbor's faults and follies."

name: Lloyd Worrall
major: Graduate Students; B.S. Ag., M. S. Ag.

name: Adams, Virgil Emory
motto: “A lawyer’s mule, of a slow pace, will both suit my disposition and business.”

name: Adamson, Ernie
motto: “He talks as if he had traveled all the prince’s courts of Christendom.”

name: Avera, Willis Moses
motto: “Come on, sir, I will lay the law to you.”

name: Ulrich J. Bennett
motto: "He carries his face in a ruff,—monstrous steady, for fear of breaking."

name: John Lamar Booth
motto: "Man was made to talk."

name: Luther Hoyt Boswell
motto: "Necessity knows no law. I know some lawyers of the name."

name: Simon Turner Brewton
motto: "Ef anything's foolisher and more 'dicklus than Militerry glory, it is Milishy glory."

name: Walter H. Burt
motto: "The first thing we do, let's kill all the lawyers."

name: Samuel Adams Cann
motto: "The normal instincks of mankind is not to work."

name: Carter, Frank
motto: "A politician is the devil's quilted anvil."

name: Chappell, Bentley Hart
motto: "The sex is ever to a soldier kind."

name: Cocke, Isaac Perry
motto: "'Tis better to be fortunate than wise."

name: John Martin Cowart
motto: "What a strange creature."

name: David Robert Cumming
motto: "Wilt hear some of my court wisdom?"

name: Carlton Carlisle Davis
motto: "Honored and blessed be the evergreen pine."

name: John Prior Edwards
motto: "Let gentleness my strong enforcement be."

name: Walker Reynolds Flournoy
motto: "My hair is white, but not with years."

name: Hugh Nelson Fuller
motto: "The Wall flower,—the Wall flower, How beautifully it blooms."

name: Gannon, Hugh Mitchell
motto: "For too much rest itself becomes a pain."

name: Gunn, Robert Russell
motto: "Let's see what the urchin's fit for."

name: Kenyon, Edgar D.
motto: "I'm great on sagacity."

name: Allen Napoleon Kieffer
motto: "Imagination rules the world."

name: Jay Weiss Kieve
motto: "Helpless as spilled beans on a dresser."

name: Emil Watson Leard
motto: "All things here are out of joint."

name: Will King Meadow
motto: "Bills! Bills! Bills! How can a man name his son William? "

name: Francis Hamilton Miller
motto: "As noiseless as a pair of empty slippers."

name: John Edward McDonald
motto: "Let's to billiards."

name: Walter Felix McDaniel
motto: "Art thou a magistrate?"

name: Walter Raleigh McDonald
motto: "A happy soul, that all the way, To heaven hath a summer's day."

name: Olin Stephen Pace
motto: "Thou art fairer than the evening air, Clad in the beauty of a thousand stars."

name: Charles Orville Parker
motto: "Little chimneys do ever cast most smoke."

name: David Roscoe Peacock
motto: "I am monarch of all I survey."

name: Edgar Legare Pennington
motto: "Listen to William W. Shakespeare."

name: Ivey Washington Rountree
motto: "This man by his apparel, Some men would judge a politician."

name: Henry Dozier Russell
motto: "Come, you and I are friends."

name: Francis Walker Salley
motto: "Of all the girls that are so smart, There's none like pretty Sally."

name: Edward Lowe Semple
motto: "Even kebbidge-heads hez rights."

name: Augustus Octavius Bacon Sparks
motto: "An English tailor, crazed i' the brain, With the study of new fashions."

name: Clarence Rowland Vaughn
motto: "We shall not see his like again."

name: Victor Victor
motto: "I am persuaded that every thing advances by an unchangeable law through the eternal CONSTITUTION."

name: Ernie Adamson
description: Adamson, or "Ernie" as he is sometimes called, joined the merry chase for a diploma last year some weeks after the starter's pistol had fired. Nevertheless, he has heard numerous shots since that time, some of which were directed at him. Since entering the Law School, Adamson has made the acquaintance of Mr. Morris, become possessed of several law books, attended most of the shows at the Colonial, and has shown remarkable proficiency in attending two classes at once. Victor is his partner in this organization.

name: William King Meadow
enrolltime: some time after the "War Between the States"
description: Escaped from a circus menagerie some time after the "War Between the States", and has been a student at the University ever since. But withal, Bill is a youth in years. He is a remarkably intelligent person, being able to actually read and write a little; is able to distinguish between arson and forgery; corporeal hereditaments and nuisances; professors and janitors; and between John Edwards and an Egyptian mummy. He was prominent in military circles, having risen from private of the worst and most verdant description to Cadet Major. War clouds on the Mexican border induced him to hastily withdraw from the service this year in favor of Chappell. Bentley can run faster of the two. But this is no indication that Bill is not possessed of most wonderful nerve. On the contrary, he is composed chiefly of that element. To bear out his reputation for courageousness and valor, he roomed with Sparks, sat on the front row in class, raved at baseball umpires in the manner of a militant suffragette before the House of Parliament, went to church on Sunday mornings, undertook to serve as President of his class, took a meal at the "Beanery", rode on the Gainesville Midland Railway once, often ridiculed "Boss" Russell, and will probably practice law with "Evergreen" Davis.

name: Dick Russell
enrolltime: from a time when the memory of man (or woman) runneth not to the contrary
description: It will be hard for us to sum up in a few words all that we feel concerning Russell. He has been at the University from a time when the memory of man (or woman) runneth not to the contrary, and hath become a fixture on the campus and one of the necessary appurtenances to Sanford Field. While at the University Dick took exercise at the "Beanery," was King of Graftdom, a wonderful ape at all athletic contests, was possessed sui juris of an inestimable supply of Bull, and was one of the most popular malefactors who has ever graced (?) the campus. As "boss" his jurisdiction extended to the Freshmen by authority of statute, and through the exercise of his reserve power his chancery jurisdiction was extended to all other classes and to the faculty. During his long term here he has become partly civilized—learning to wear a collar on occasions of State and at church,—smoked once, learned to wear green

name: EDGAR LEGARE PENNINGTON
description: A genius and hence doomed to either the poor house or the penitentiary. Well versed in all literary spheres, and will soon give to the world his theory as to the author of Shakespearian plays. He will also publish an article showing how the "Tango" and other like contortions were originated by the Adamites and Pre-Adamites, and, if there was anything back of them, by them. He speaks various languages, to wit, Anglo-Saxon, Hebrew, Latin, Greek, Dago and United States. He has never become acquainted with the rudiments of the English or profane languages. While here Edgar never learned to chew, nor did he smoke, nor drink, nor did he inflict his presence upon any fair one. He was studious at all times, and utilized his spare moments in composing mouth organ music and love ballads.

name: VICTOR VICTOR
nicknames: The Walking Encyclopedia of Universal Knowledge
enrolltime: During his sojourn at the University
description: This rare intellectual gem has been better known among us as "The Walking Encyclopedia of Universal Knowledge," being little less well versed in the highest and most useless arts than is Pennington. During his sojourn at the University, Victor performed many remarkable feats, the most remarkable by far being his arrival in class on time on the morning of January 8th, in the year of our Lord One Thousand Nine Hundred and Fourteen. Mr. Morris and the class marveled exceedingly, and it was declared by the "powers that be" that in the event such an unbelievable thing should again occur, a holiday would be declared. We have never had the holiday. While in Athens, Victor was a member of the Standard Pressing Club, and also a member of the National Federation of Bootlicks. We predict for him a great success as a vaudeville actor.

name: CINCINNATUS RUBENSTEIN VAUGHN
description: The "Conyers Beauty"—a dainty little creature of a most "AMY-able" disposition. "Rube" avers on oath that he is from "Conyers, Georgia, on the main line of the Georgia Railroad from Atlanta to Augusta." Just to take a slant at him you would suppose that Conyers is "NEAR" said Georgia Railroad, but we always took his word for it. He always went to all the circuses, ate peanuts, associated with Davis and Russell, and other crimes and misdemeanors here and there, now and then, with malice aforethought committed against the peace, dignity and good order of the State and the citizens thereof. He was accustomed, when undisturbed by pins, to abide in blissful slumber each day between the hours of twelve and one.

name: CHARLES ORVILLE PARKER
enrolltime: He will leave the University this year
description: About the worst thing we can say is that Charlie is from South Georgia, and that he associates with Bob Gunn. He hails from that section of the "Empire State" known as the "Wiregrass" region. However, he does not look so very "grassy". Gabe Cowart has been leading him out—or probably misleading him—and he has a chance. He is renowned for his superb beauty, his gross ignorance, his ravenous appetite and his charming voice. He will leave the University this year we suppose, and ought to have a fair knowledge of where the law can be found if, unhappily, he has not been with Adamson too much.

name: WALTER FELIX McDANIEL
description: Another animal of prehistoric times from South Georgia—almost as bad as Charlie Parker. He is quiet and unassuming; in fact, almost too quiet at times when called upon in class. He almost always remains behind the goal posts in class room, but comes to the front and does spectacular work. His connection with a popular hotel has given him a wide acquaintance among traveling salesmen and "Show" people. Give him a soap box, a Code of Georgia and the necessary crossing of the roads, and he will make an admirable success in the field of law somewhere in the woods of Georgia.

name: Semple, Eddie
description: A most peculiar specimen of humanity—we suppose it is humanity—from the Everglades of Florida. He was brought here last year by process of extradition, and has served his sentence well. His association with the Indians of Southern Florida has evidently made a deep impression upon him, for he still insists at times upon wearing red plumes as headgear. But he is not unlike other students. He runs around on two legs, eats food and drinks water. Since being in the "Classic" city he has learned to ride on street cars and has become very accomplished with the cue. He was occasionally observed in the class room, and always had a seat of honor near Bill Avera in the "Bootlick" row. Eddie gave much promise of becoming a regular gambler when he first came to college, actually betting and winning two bits on a Tech. game, but since that time he has reformed and his worst offenses at present are smoking "Threefors" and cutting Sunday school classes.

name: Salley, Frank
description: Formerly a resident of Augusta—now of College Avenue. A marvelous theatrical product he is in many ways, and in many other ways not unlike the apes and gorillas of darkest Africa. A great many of our boys operated private "grafts", but Frank feloniously and maliciously maintained a public nuisance on College Avenue, to wit: the Classic Theater, much to the detriment of the public in general. His extreme timidity in the presence of femmes soles is indeed painful, but the ease with which he bore up under a fire of questions in class room did much to cause us to overlook his shortcomings. He is eminently well fitted to become a dog and pony trainer with Barnum & Bailey, or the stage manager for just any kind of vaudeville company.

name: Flournoy, Walker Reynolds
description: Hair, white; complexion, fair; feet, LARGE; brain, cloudy; eyes, faded blue; habits, fairly good; nerve, absolutely unlimited. The above is a description of Walker Reynolds, clearly, fully, plainly and distinctly set forth—without superfluous allegations of matters of an irrelevant or immaterial nature. He is an author of note,—limited note, however. He has written during his college career the following: "The Intricacies of Common Law Pleading Simplified." "Who Can be Found By the Terms of a Void Contract." "Equity and Its Application in Africa and New England." "Form Book Practice in Georgia and Its Advantages." All of these books may be found in manuscript form in the boiler room under the Academic building,—absolutely unreadable and equally incombustible. Unless the General Assembly passes a law forbidding impositions upon helpless clients, he will enter the practice with his Code, the Form Book, his unlimited nerve and congenial habits.

name: Burt
description: One of the "Freshman" members of the class. We do not mean that he is possessed of those propensities common to Freshmen, but that he is of the Kenyon-Bennett-Taylor-Burt aggregation, with which crowd we have been afflicted only one year. Burt is one of these "long, tall, and rangy guys", with a face composed chiefly of nose. He is rather quiet, but when he speaks he roars as Zeus speaking from Olympus. Whatever may be his knowledge of law, which appears to be vast even at this time, he will be able to frighten the ordinary jury into rendering a verdict in his favor. Much discomfort was created among the peaceful citizens of Buena Vista—his home town—when it was rumored that he would return there and practice law, but this agitation has now been allayed for Burt has decided to accept the Presidency of the Atlanta School of Law. As a professor ? ? ? ? ?

name: Young, James Wilson
description: The rosy-cheeked son of the Blue Ridge. Tried to get to Athens two years ago via the Gainesville Midland, but found it more advantageous to walk. Has read more cases than the entire class.

name: JAY WEISS KIEVE
description: You'll have to admit that Jay Weiss Kieve is the most dickens of a name. But then the name hasn't got anything on Kieve. Kieve can help run a picture show, go to law school, and whistle like a mockingbird. At class he sits up on the port side of the altar. He used to sit on the other side, but had to move over on account of the knot hole in the platform. Kieve has had several great honors while here, among them being Frank Salley's assistant and Sheriff of Moot Court.

name: EMIL WATSON LEARD
description: Leard plays chess, the violin and the deuce. He has an A.B. degree from Mercer, a medal which he won at Hartwell, and the oldest man in college for a roommate. Leard sings in the Methodist choir, tries for inter-collegiate debates and on the whole manages to spend the time very pleasantly without spending it all in search of the law. It is rumored that Leard is in love, and in fact, some say that he is the guy that put heart in Hartwell, but from good authority, we understand that Bowman, Ga., is the favored (?) town.

name: WALTER RALEIGH McDONALD
description: W. R. McDonald, law student, lover, business man, and hot-air artist. Between "Old Man" Adams and Olin Wimberly, Mc gets led a merry chase. "Old Man" tries to get Mc to bed by 9 o'clock and Olin tries to keep him up till six. When it was suggested that we study a $13 book on Real Property, Mc had great fits of glee. You see, Mc sells the books. And Mc is some lover, too, making frequent unnecessary trips to Augusta, and occasionally getting in the hospital. But then all is fair, etc. Mc is also a member of the Ten O'Clock Club, rooms in the Ben Franklin Annex, and on the whole is quite a famous man. His twenty-first birthday party will go down in history.

name: JOHN EDWARD McDONALD
enrolltime: matriculated at Georgia away back about the time that they used to have to run the Indians out of the Chapel every morning
description: "Jay Bird" McDonald first matriculated at Georgia away back about the time that they used to have to run the Indians out of the Chapel every morning in order to have the exercises. But he decided to wait a while and pick out the best class possible with which to get his B.L. degree. Jay Bird has been taking all the law course and about seven-eighths of the Academic, and yet has had time to join in the festivities around the Q Room occasionally. Jay Bird knows most of the buildings on the campus by name, and has been all over the Law department, with the possible exception of the Library. Jay Bird may not be a very dignified name for an Attorney and Counsellor at Law, but then all attorneys can't be dignified, you know.

name: FRANCIS HAMILTON MILLER
description: "Hamp" Miller looks like a lineal descendant of the Sphinx related in the 'steenth degree as calculated by the civil law. Hamp is certainly a tranquil member. He is so peaceful that with a slight stretch of the imagination, you can see doves of peace hovering about him. Hamp is as regular as—well, nearly as regular as Dr. Morris. He moves by schedule, and is always on time. Hamp is a very temperate boy, his worst and most vicious habit being that obnoxious and contemptible custom of going to Augusta on slight provocation.

name: EUGENE S. TAYLOR
description: 'Gene Taylor is another member of the Grandfather's Club. When he first infested the Campus is yet a query. If you ever heard anything about law being dry, you just want to see 'Gene Taylor at...

name: DAVID ROSCOE PEACOCK
clubs: Captain of last year's football team
President of his Freshman Class (the Class of '13)
description: "Emp", as you know, was Captain of last year's football team; however, we expect him to get his diploma. "Emp" has had grievous obstacles to overcome. "Bish" Brewton and Carter have been in his class for 5 years. And "Bish" has been leading him a merry chase down Lover's Lane, too. So between "Bish", and Dr. Morris, and the football team, and the Standard Pressing Club, "Emp" has done well to survive. Peacock has many college honors.

name: OLIN STEPHEN PACE
description: Steve Pace had no opposition for Junior President, per quod he was elected unanimously. For a while this year we feared we'd lose Steve. It looked very much like he was contemplating the Third Title, Chapter 1, Article 1, Section 1, of the Civil Code of Georgia, but Steve is still with us contrary to our expectations. He is an ardent member of the "Ten O'Clock Club", hikes for Brenau on slight provocation and a dollar or two, and at odd times may be found in the Library trying in vain to keep up with Mr. Green's numerous cases. Pace had rather get up in the Library with his collar and coat off, whistle and kick over chairs and read the head notes of a lot of cases, than to be picking the proverbial harp.

name: SIMON TURNER BREWTON
motto: "With malice to none, with charity for all."
description: "Bish" Brewton exemplifies, by his life, the great principle enunciated by Abraham Lincoln, as follows, to wit: "With malice to none, with charity for all." His besetting sin is the unbounded pride which he takes in his own good looks. It is probable that this arises from his extensive acquaintance with the fair sex and his ability to see the false flattery used by said fair sex. It has been intimated that he is somewhat of a boot-licker, but as he confines his efforts to attempts to increase his popularity with the aforesaid fair sex by lending aid to other members of the family, the class, as a whole, has not classified him as one of the smooth-tongued men.

name: ULRIC J. BENNETT
clubs: manager of a farm in and about Maxeys, Ga.
description: Ulric J. Bennett is a noble example of that great class of men who spoil a good farmer when they enter the legal profession. As manager of a farm in and about Maxeys, Ga., he could possibly have earned a livelihood and risen to the rank of J. P., thereby satisfying his double ambition of supporting himself and winning renown in the legal profession, but as a lawyer, we doubt that he will ever accomplish either.

name: DAVID ROBERT CUMMING
description: No, Cumming, D.R., isn't hanging on until he qualifies for a place as a Carnegie Fund Beneficiary. It is true that he has only a short time longer to serve. His sole object in remaining in the University thus long is to permit the first idea with which he came in contact to penetrate his ivory dome. The serious expression worn by him is not a sign of deep thinking, but is only the sham behind which he conceals his ignorance. Some go so far as to intimate that he is "conscientiously" opposed to knowing anything.

name: JOHN PRIOR EDWARDS
description: The only thing legal about "J. P." Edwards is his initials. It has been said that the initials in question were given him in order that he might have a natural advantage over the other members of his class.

name: J. P. Edwards
description: No, the wild look, the hair standing on end, these are not signs of reversion to the original type, but only the effect of his childhood environment. He hails from the town of jungles, panthers, tigers and giraffes. Rumor has it that he once saw a train before he came to Athens. But he is not altogether devoid of intelligence. In fact, we believe that when he dies, his epitaph may be truly written: J. P. Edwards, J. P.

name: CARLTON CARLISLE DAVIS
motto: "How long, O Catiline, wilt thou abuse our patience? How long will Providence spare thee and permit us to suffer?"
clubs: National Board of Censorship at all picture shows
description: At the mention of the above name we invariably exclaim: \"How long, O Catiline, wilt thou abuse our patience? How long will Providence spare thee and permit us to suffer?\" \"Evergreen\" best expresses all that there is to him. Winter, fall, summer and baseball season always find him the same ever verdant one. He won the distinction of being the biggest Freshman in college last year, and ran an excellent race this year. An excellent example of sound judgment on the part of the common people. While at the University, Davis was seen at class on one or two occasions. He acted as the National Board of Censorship at all picture shows, and was an unmovable fixture at Poss' Place. He liked every man in college with the exception of Vaughn and Middlebrooks,—he simply loved them. We predict the gallows, the insane asylum, or the Federal Prison for him at an early date.

name: IVEY WASHINGTON ROUNTREE
description: Even like unto the father of his country, whose name he bears, he could not—but then, he never cut down a cherry tree. Like Teddy Roosevelt he stirred politics with a big stick, and he may be President some of these days. Davis declares that Abe Lincoln was President of this country once,—why not Rountree? While in the University, Rountree devoted considerable time in making investigation concerning matters of legal and illegal nature. The remainder of his time was spent in asking questions which were calculated to convey the idea that he knew something. Otherwise he ATE, talked, looked and almost acted like a human being. In the court room he is calculated to cause considerable comment among the \"Niggers,\" as will Russell. We predict much success for him because of the fact that he is a Progressive, and is from South Georgia.

name: GUS SPARKS
motto: "Of utterly depraved and abandoned mind, an enemy to the race and to civilization."
description: A gentleman who defamed his worthy name by entering the ranks of journalism, and was, as a certain noted authority has put it: \"Of utterly depraved and abandoned mind, an enemy to the race and to civilization.\" Bill Meadow, his roommate by misfortune, construed the above to mean he (Bill) was both the race and civilization. Augustus invariably reported to class late, but that same gentleness of spirit and fluency of speech that won for him a place among the \"Kings of Suffragettes\", was sufficient to make good his every shortcoming or latecoming, as it is in this case. He was a bona fide holder of a corn cob pipe (of some considerable strength), which pipe he often negotiated to Bill; a person sui juris (probably); one non compos mentis (possibly); a moderate grafter; an exponent of temperance; and one of the few creatures of God's creation who believes that Macon is a city.

name: WILLIS MOSES AVERA
description: Bill Avery insists that he has never given Blackstone any serious consideration. We believe Bill to be a truthful Boy. So does Mr. Morris. Bill sits on the front seat in all his classes, is always present at the meeting of the \"Ten O'clock Club,\" which meets at Poss' Place every morning. He rarely studies later than 3 o'clock A. M. Several times Bill has been known to answer a question correctly and is found in the Law Library on rare occasions. Each Professor insists on pronouncing Avera different, hence Bill will answer to either 'A-very, Av-e'ra, or Av-e-ra'.

name: VIRGIL EMORY ADAMS
description: “Old Man” Adams is a great old scout. He is a consistent worker, a debater of no mean ability, and a Sunday School enthusiast. He has never cracked a text-book on Sunday, but has been heard to

name: JOHN LAMAR BOOTH
description: John is a native of the Classic City, and also has an inexhaustible supply of questions on old Exams. Hence, he is the most popular man in the class just before an Examination. He wears glasses, eats with a knife and fork, and is otherwise very much like other human beings. He knows more about mules than any other 7 men in the class, but nevertheless can drive his automobile as well as a mule trade. At the present writing, no member of the class has yet seen John drinking out of the same cup with any member of the faculty; we predict that he will pull down the much coveted diploma entitling him to "plead and practice law" in the courts of Georgia.

name: LUTHER HOYT BOSWELL
description: So far as we know, Hoyt possesses none of the vices of a gentleman; but notwithstanding this fact, he is. However he rooms with Dick Russell, reads the "Sunday American", "The Ginger Jar," "Snappy Stories", besides "Shipman's Common Law Pleading," and other light literature. Hoyt is an enthusiastic member of the "Ten O'clock Club," frequently entertains at Cue Parties, and on the whole, has managed to spend his time and incidentally some money very pleasantly while waiting for a dip.

name: WILLIAM LeGRAND BRYAN, A.B.
motto: By Gosh
description: We pray for inspiration as we attempt to tell you of Wm. L. Bryan. To appreciate the man, you must do more than merely turn the pages of this book to find his picture and gaze into those sparkling eyes. The "Secretary" is not really 74 years old as many suppose, but only 47. He has graduated from Emory College and taught school in every County from Jesup to Rockmart. He is so punctual that it is painful. He has never been absent from a class in the whole two years. No, not even tardy—refuses to answer "Here", but insists on responding to roll call very politely, to wit: "Present". Secretary eats one meal a day, and drinks that out of a milk bottle, wears his hair polar bear style, and has never been shot (nor even half shot). In fact Bryan is always perfectly peaceful, presumably present, and painfully precise.

name: SAMUEL ADAMS CANN
clubs: Chef de Cuisine de la Cafe Pulaski
description: Sam Cann is a carefree, congenial, ball-headed old boy. On January 8th and March 14th he got to Mr. Morris' class on time. All the other days he walked in calmly after roll call and requested sheepishly, "Mark Cann present, please, sir". Bob Gunn said that when Mr. Morris addressed the Y. M. C. A., he couldn't listen to the lecture for fear Cann would come in and ask to be marked present. Sam has been to Europe. If you don't believe it, ask Sparks. Sam's greatest college honors are as follows: Member of the Piedmont Football Team; Member "Ten O'clock Club", and Chef de Cuisine de la Cafe Pulaski. Sam's O. K., and he's strong for Herpicide.

name: FRANK CARTER
description: Frank is Ed's brother. He eats nearly as much as Ed, gets put out of basket-ball games more, and is ex-manager of the baseball team. Frank is a great lover, but says that he believes that variety is the spice of life. Carter and Steve Pace can get in the library and think they are reading cases and make more fuss than a sheet iron band. A dollar has no more chance around Carter than that proverbial celluloid dog chasing the asbestos cat through the hottest part of Hades.

name: BENTLEY HART CHAPPELL
description: "Chap" spends his time as follows: One-third drawing up his papers neatly, one-third drilling, and the other third in looking over his papers to see if he has made any mistakes. Bentley is Cadet

name: ISAAC PERRY COCKE
clubs: S. P. C. S. (Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Students)
description: Bet you can't guess why he is named Isaac. Perry Cocke entered the University in 1908. However, he hasn't been here continuously ever since, like some people I know. Perry is quite a clubman. He belongs to the "Manifold Club", the "Ten O'clock Club", the "Midnight Owls", and the S. P. C. S. (Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Students). Perry is kinder hard to describe, but you have seen these fellows that were always present when anything happened, haven't you? Well, Perry has been there every time anything has happened since he has been here. Like Bob Gunn and Bob Raiford, you know.

name: JOHN MARTIN COWART
description: "Gas" Cowart is the hottest sport in the Law Class, "Evergreen" Davis not excepted. Whenever they start to do anything over at Brenau, they send over and consult Gas, and he always has to go over to inspect the proposition first hand. Pace and Gunn usually go, too, but Gas is a good-natured fellow and doesn't get mad. Gas would die if he couldn't prop his feet up on the stove in Mr. Morris' class room. Gas says that he may not be so much for speed, but when it comes to endurance, he is the Three Year King.

name: WALTER ERLE DALEY
description: Erle Daley lives in Atlanta. But that's nothing; Hugh Fuller does, too. But then Daley has a mustache. When Erle starts to tell Mr. Morris some startling fact, for example, that there are two kinds of chairs, to wit: straight chairs and rocking chairs, he leans back in his seat, hangs his thumb in the arm-hole of his vest and begins like he was going to deliver a Fourth of July Oration on the "Blood Bought Heritage of the Constitution", but ere he has finished you are firmly convinced that there really is such a classification of chairs as he suggested. And if he doesn't convince you, you at least believe that he's in earnest. The reason that they call him Daley is because he is not (daily).

name: HUGH NELSON FULLER
description: Hugh Fuller lives in Atlanta. But that's nothing; Erle Daley does, too. But Fuller has been "Borie" at Emory and "Vice-Borie" at Harvard, but he couldn't even get nominated for "Borie" over here. Nevertheless, Fuller is on the job, and he has an office in the Government building, too. Here he reads "Life", keeps his razor, etc., smokes Judge Cornett's tobacco, and generally makes himself a nuisance, no doubt. He is supposed to be a Government official of some kind, but nobody knows what Hugh's official capacity is. Maybe he's Government Chicken Inspector.

name: HUGH MITCHELL GANNON
description: "Hank" Gannon is a long-legged old boy that wears specks, loves to have a good time, and rooms with MacHutton. "Hank" and W. R. McDonald delight in canvassing the city for advertisements for the "Red and Black", and they say that Gannon's spiel is irresistible. Hugh is a desperate lover, a horrible singer, and a pseudo-lawyer of no mean ability.

name: ROBERT RUSSELL GUNN
description: There's no use to say anything about Bob Gun. Of course, you know Bob Gun. If you don't, you may be sure that he knows you. Bob is president of the German Club, and is otherwise the social leader of the University. If Bob can just get to Brenau, he is happy. In fact Bob knows every student at Lucy Cobb, Brenau, Agnes Scott, Wesleyan, and the Crawfordville High School. The number of girls Bob knows is greater than the number of Georgia boys that "Ike" knows. And the funny part about the whole thing is that Bob has actually been at work this year, and on top of it all, he lives in Crawfordville, where he says he is going to be a corporation lawyer.

name: ALLEN NAPOLEON KIEFER
description: "Keef" had rather pin a piece of paper on somebody's coat tail than be Chancellor of the University, or Dean of the Law School. It has been said that he is the biggest Freshman in the Law Class. "Keef" says that he doesn't think Evergreen Davis is a married woman nor an infant, but when it comes to this non compos mentis business, he doesn't want to take any oaths. "Keef" has an A.B. degree which he obtained over in Newberry, S. C., a very cheerful disposition, and a face that would stop a Big Ben Alarm Clock, as well as a very unusual flow of big words, and Rubenstein Vaughn for a roommate. In spite of all these disadvantages, together with the fact that such a small man has to drag around such a name as you see written above, he has made a very good record, and we predict that some day he will be a Justice Court Solicitor, if he works hard.

name: EDGAR L. KENYON
description: If there is anything that you want to know about, from the amount of snow fall at Dahlonega to the temperature of the shoot-the-chutes at Warm Springs, ask Ed Kenyon. He's an Emory boy. But Ed is hardly a boy—he's 'most an old man. When we had the spring holidays, Ed, "Old Man" Adams, "Secretary" Bryan, "Fes" Cummings, and Ulric Bennett went up to Atlanta to celebrate. Ed led this gay and giddy crowd of striplings. They bought chewing gum, chocolate milks, pop-corn, went to the movies, and had a big time in general. Bennett says that he isn't going off with Kenyon any more, because he kept on telling the soda jerker not to put too much chocolate in the chocolate milks, as he didn't want to have to take anybody home. But Ed is some lawyer. Bob Gunn exclaimed at Jeffersonian the night that Ed demurred a certain petition out of court, "Good gosh, fellows, I'm going to get 40 counties away from that man when I start to practice law."

name: J. F. Hammett
clubs: President of the Senior Pharmacy Class

name: M. E. Freeman
clubs: Vice-President of the Senior Pharmacy Class

name: R. W. Murray
clubs: Secretary of the Senior Pharmacy Class

name: George Harrison
clubs: Historian of the Senior Pharmacy Class

name: J. P. Wingate
clubs: Chaplain of the Senior Pharmacy Class

name: Murray Elton Freeman, Ph.G.
motto: "I go to mix the powders, That send my friends below."

name: Frank Dorsey Gray, Ph.G.
motto: "When taken, To be well shaken."

name: George L. Harrison, Ph.G.
motto: "On their own merits modest men are dumb."

name: Robert Walker Murray
motto: "He will shoot pills."

name: H. D. Williams
motto: "The dimple that thy chin contains has beauty in its round."

name: James P. Wingate
motto: "So modern 'pothecaries Prescribe, apply, and call their masters fools."

name: Atkisson, George Baber
clubs: Private in Troup Artillery, C. S. Army
description: Disabled in Battle, Sharpsburg, Md. Entered for A.B. course.

name: Briscoe, Matthew P.
description: Junior in A.B. course.

name: Belcher, John Hampden
clubs: Adj. 1st. S. C. Cav., C. S. Army
description: Senior in A.B. course.

name: Belcher, James Norwood
clubs: 1st S. C. Cav., C. S. A.

name: Jackson, James Marion
clubs: Capt. in 11th Ga. Regt., C. S. A.

name: Middlebrooks, William M.
clubs: Capt. 19th Ga. Regt., C. S. Army

name: Stoddard, John I.
clubs: Capt. and A. D. D., C. S. A.
description: Joined Co. B., Ga. "Hussars."

name: Thomas, James Dudley
clubs: Private Troup Artillery, C. S. A.

name: Weed, Edwin Gardener
description: Rt. Rev. Weed, Edwin Gardener.

name: John Nicholson
description: As historian of the Class of 1916, my task is easy. There are so many good traits that the only difficulty is to select from them those which should be set forth; the bad ones on the other hand are so few that it is no trouble to omit them.

name: BIDEZ, ERNEST BASS
motto: ,name:
